modules.datatypes package#

Submodules#

modules.datatypes.contour module#

class Contour(name: str, traces: list[Trace] = None)[source]#

Bases: object

append(trace: Trace)[source]#

Append a trace to the existing traces.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to append to the contour

copy()[source]#

Return a copy of the contour.

getBounds(tform=None)[source]#

Get the bounds of the contour.

Params:

tform (Transform): the transform to apply to the traces

Returns:

(tuple): the (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) extremes for the contours

getMidpoint(tform=None)[source]#

Get the midpoint of the contour (average of extremes).

Params:

tform (Transform): the transform to apply to the traces

Returns:

(tuple): the x, y coords for the midpoint

getTraces() → list[source]#

Return the list of traces.

importTraces(other, threshold: float = 0.95)[source]#

Import all of the traces from another contour.

Params:

other (Contour): the contour with traces to import threshold (float): the overlap threshold

index(trace: Trace)[source]#

Find the index of a trace in the list.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to find the index for

isEmpty() → bool[source]#

Return True if no traces associated with contour.

remove(trace: Trace)[source]#

Remove a trace from the list.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to remove from the contour

modules.datatypes.flag module#

class Comment(user: str, text: str, date: str = None, time: str = None)[source]#

Bases: object

copy()[source]#

Returns a copy of the current flag comment.

fromList()[source]#

Create a flag comment object from a list.

Params:

l (list): the list

getList() → list[source]#

Get the flag comment object as a list.

class Flag(name: str, x: int, y: int, section_number: int, color: tuple, comments=None, resolved=False, id=None)[source]#

Bases: object

addComment(user: str, text: str)[source]#

Add a comment to the flag.

Params:

user (str): the user who created the comment text (str): the comment text

copy()[source]#

Returns a copy of the current flag.

equals(other)[source]#

Compare flag objects.

Params:

other (Flag): the flag to compare to

fromList(snum: int)[source]#

Create a flag object from a list.

Params:

l (list): the list snum (int): the section containing the flag

generateID()[source]#

Generate an ID for a flag.

getList() → list[source]#

Returns the flag in a list representation.

resolve(user: str, resolved=True)[source]#

Resolve or unresolve the flag.

Params:

user (str): the user that is modifying the flag resolved (bool): the resolve status for the flag

getDateTime()[source]#

modules.datatypes.log module#

class Log(date: str, time: str, user: str, obj_name: str, section, event: str)[source]#

Bases: object

addSection(snum: int)[source]#

Add a section number to the section range.

Params:

snum (int) the section number to add

checkSectionRanges()[source]#

Iterate through the section ranges and combine adjacent ones.

containsSection(snum: int)[source]#

Check if the logs section range contains the given section number.

Params:

snum (int): the section number

fromStr()[source]#

Get a log object from a string.

Params:

s (str): the string

trimSectionRange(srange)[source]#

Trim the section range of the log.

class LogSet[source]#

Bases: object

addExistingLog(log: Log, track_dyn=False)[source]#

Add an existing log object to the set.

Params:

log (Log): the log to add to the set track_dyn (bool): True if log should be dynamically tracked

addLog(user: str, obj_name: str, snum: int, event: str)[source]#

Add a log to the set.

Params:

user (str): the user creating the log obj_name (str): the name of the object associated with the log snum (int): the section number associated with the log event (str): the description of the event

fromList()[source]#

Get a log set from a list.

Params:

log_list (list): the list representation of the logs

getLastIndex(snum: int, cname: str)[source]#

Scan the history and return the date for a contour on a given section.

Params:

snum (int): the section number cname (str): the contour name

getList() → list[source]#

Return log set as a list

getLogList(as_str=False)[source]#

Return the stored logs as a list.

Params:

as_str (bool): True if the log should be returned in str format

Returns:

the log list in list or str format

removeCuration(obj_name: str)[source]#

Remove all curation logs in the session.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to remove curation for

class LogSetPair(logset0: LogSet, logset1: LogSet)[source]#

Bases: object

importLogs(series, traces=True, ztraces=True, srange=None, regex_filters=[])[source]#

Import the history data from the other logset into the series logset

Params:

series (Series): the series to modify the history for traces (bool): True if trace history should be imported ztraces (bool): True if the ztrace history should be imported srange (tuple): the range of sections (exclusive) regex_filters (list): the list of regex filters used to filter names

getDateTime()[source]#

modules.datatypes.obj_group_dict module#

class ObjGroupDict(groups: dict = None)[source]#

Bases: object

add(group: str, obj: str)[source]#

Add items to the two-way dictionary.

Params:

group (str): the group obj (str): the object to add to the group

copy()[source]#

Return a copy of the two-way dict.

getGroupDict() → dict[source]#

Get a JSON serializable dictionary.

getGroupList() → list[source]#

Get a list of groups.

getGroupObjects(group: str) → set[source]#

Get the objects for a given group.

Params:

group (str): the group to get objects for

Returns:

(set): the objects associated with the group

getObjectGroups(obj: str = None) → set[source]#

Get the groups for a given object.

Params:

obj (str): the object to get the groups for

Returns:

(set): the groups associated with the obj

getObjectList() → list[source]#

Get a list of objects.

merge(other, regex_filters=[])[source]#

Merge other object group dict into self.

remove(group: str, obj: str)[source]#

Remove items from the two-way dictionary.

Params:

group (str): the group obj (str): the object to remove from the group

removeGroup(group: str)[source]#

Remove a group.

Params:

groups (str): the group to remove

removeObject(obj: str)[source]#

Remove an object from all its associated groups.

Params:

obj (str): the name of the object

renameGroup(group: str, new_group: str)[source]#

Rename a group.

Params:

group (str): the group to rename new_group (str): the new name for the group

modules.datatypes.objects module#

class Objects(series)[source]#

Bases: object

exportCSV(out_fp: str = None)[source]#

Export a CSV containing the quantitative data for all objects.

Params:

out_fp (str): the filepath for the newly created CSV (function returns str if filepath not provided)

getNames()[source]#

Return all of the object names.

modules.datatypes.section module#

class Section(n: int, series)[source]#

Bases: object

addFlag(flag: Flag, log_event=True)[source]#

Add a flag to the section.

Params:

flag (Flag): the flag to add to the section log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

addTrace(trace: Trace, log_event=True)[source]#

Add a trace to the trace dictionary.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to add log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

clearTracking()[source]#

Clear the added_traces and removed_traces lists.

deleteTraces(traces: list = None, flags: list = None, log_event=True)[source]#

Delete selected traces.

Params:

traces (list): a list of traces to delete (default is selected traces) flags (list): a list of flags to delete (default is selected flags) log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

deselectAllTraces()[source]#

Deselect all traces.

(Only meant for GUI use.)

editTraceAttributes(traces: list[Trace], name: str, color: tuple, tags: set, mode: tuple, add_tags=False, log_event=True)[source]#

Change the name and/or color of a trace or set of traces.

Params:

traces (list): the list of traces to modify name (str): the new name color (tuple): the new color tags (set): the new set of tags mode (tuple): the new fill mode for the traces add_tags (bool): True if tags should be added (rather than replaced) log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

editTraceRadius(traces: list[Trace], new_rad: float, log_event=True)[source]#

Change the radius of a trace or set of traces.

Params:

traces (list): the list of traces to change new_rad (float): the new radius for the trace(s) log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

editTraceShape(traces: list[Trace], new_shape: list, log_event=True)[source]#

Change the shape of a trace or set of traces.

Params:

traces (list): the list of traces to change new_shape (list): the new shape for the trace(s) log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

findClosest(field_x: float, field_y: float, radius=0.5, traces_in_view: list[Trace] = None, include_hidden=False)[source]#

Find closest trace/ztrace to field coordinates in a given radius.

(Only meant for GUI use.)

Params:

field_x (float): x coordinate of search center field_y (float): y coordinate of search center radius (float): 1/2 of the side length of search square traces_in_view (list): the traces in the window viewed by the user include_hidden (bool): True if hidden traces can be returned

Returns:

(tuple): the object closest to the point and the type None if no trace points are found within the radius

getAllModifiedNames() → set[source]#

Return the names of all the modified traces.

getDict() → dict[source]#

Convert section object into a dictionary.

Returns:

(dict) all of the compiled section data

getEmptyDict() → dict[source]#

Returns a dict representing an empty section.

hideTraces(traces: list = None, hide=True, log_event=True)[source]#

Hide traces.

(Only meant for GUI use.)

Params:

traces (list): the traces to hide hide (bool): True if traces should be hidden log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

importTraces(other, regex_filters=[], threshold: float = 0.95, flag_conflicts: bool = True, histories: LogSetPair = None, favored: str = '', dt_str: str = None)[source]#

Import the traces from another section.

Params:

other (Section): the section with traces to import regex_filters (list): the list of regex filters for objects threshold (float): the overlap threshold flag_conflicts (bool): True if conflicts should be flagged histories (LogSetPair): the self history and the other history favored (str): the favored series in the case of a conflict (“self”, “other”, or “”) dt_str (str): the datetime string for tagging purposes

makeNegative(negative=True, log_event=True)[source]#

Make a set of traces negative.

(Only meant for GUI use.)

Params:

negative (bool): the negative status of the traces to modify log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

new(snum: int, image_location: str, mag: float, thickness: float, wdir: str)[source]#

Create a new blank section file.

Params:

series_name (str): the name for the series snum (int): the section number image_location (str): the file path for the image mag (float): microns per pixel for the section thickness (float): the section thickness in microns wdir (str): the working directory for the sections

Returns:

(Section): the newly created section object

removeFlag(flag: Flag, log_event=True)[source]#

Remove a flag from the section.

Params:

flag (Flag): the flag to remove from the section log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

removeTrace(trace: Trace, log_event=True)[source]#

Remove a trace from the trace dictionary.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to remove from the traces dictionary log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

save(update_series_data=True)[source]#

Save file into json.

Params:

update_series_data (bool): True if series data object should be updated

selectAllTraces()[source]#

Select all traces.

(Only meant for GUI use.)

setAlignLocked(align_locked: bool)[source]#

Set the alignment locked status of the section.

Params:

align_locked (bool): the new locked status

setMag(new_mag: float)[source]#

Set the magnification for the section.

Params:

new_mag (float): the new magnification for the section

property tform#
tformsModified(scaling_only=False)[source]#
tracesAsList() → list[Trace][source]#

Return the trace dictionary as a list. Does NOT copy traces.

Returns:

(list): a list of traces

translateTraces(dx: float, dy: float, log_event=True)[source]#

Translate the selected traces.

Params:

dx (float): x-translate dy (float): y-translate log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

unhideAllTraces(log_event=True)[source]#

Unhide all traces on the section.

(Only meant for GUI use.)

Params:

log_event (bool): true if the event should be logged

updateJSON(n)[source]#

Add missing attributes to section JSON.

(Updates the dictionary in place)

Params:

section_data (dict): the JSON data to update n (int): the section number

modules.datatypes.series module#

class Series(filepath: str, sections: dict, get_series_data=True)[source]#

Bases: object

addLog(obj_name: str, snum: int, event: str)[source]#

Add a log to the log set.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the modified object snum (int): the section number of the event event (str): the description of the event

close()[source]#

Clear the hidden directory of the series.

createZtrace(obj_name: str, cross_sectioned: bool = True, log_event=True)[source]#

Create a ztrace from an existing object in the series.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to create the ztrace from cross_sectioned (bool): True if one ztrace point per section, False if multiple per section log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

deleteAllTraces(trace_name: str, tags: set = None, series_states=None)[source]#

Delete all traces with a certain name and tag set.

Params:

trace_name (str): the name of the traces to delete tags (set): the tags to check to delete

deleteDuplicateTraces(threshold: float, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Delete all duplicate traces in the series (keep tags).

Params:

threshold (float): the threshold for overlapping traces to be considered duplicates series_states (dict): optional dict of undo states for GUI log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

deleteObjects(obj_names: list, series_states=None)[source]#

Delete object(s) from the series.

Params:

obj_names (list): the objects to delete series_states (dict): for use with GUI states

editObjectAttributes(obj_names: list, name: str = None, color: tuple = None, tags: set = None, mode: tuple = None, sections: list = None, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Edit the attributes of objects.

Params:

obj_names (list): the names of the objects to rename name (str): the new name for the objects color (tuple): the new color for the objects tags (set): the tags to ADD to the traces of the objects mode (tuple): the display mode to set for the traces section (list): the section numbers to modify the object on (default: all) series_states: the series states as store in the GUI log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

editObjectRadius(obj_names: list, new_rad: float, series_states=None)[source]#

Change the radii of all traces of an object.

Params:

obj_names (list): the names of objects to modify new_rad (float): the new radius for the traces of the object series_states (dict): optional dict for GUI undo states

editObjectShape(obj_names: list, new_shape: list, series_states=None)[source]#

Change the shape of all traces of an object.

Params:

obj_names (list): the names of objects to modify new_shape (list): the new shape for the traces of the object series_states (dict): optional dict for GUI undo states

editZtraceAttributes(ztrace: Ztrace, name: str, color: tuple, log_event=True)[source]#

Edit the name and color of a ztrace.

Params:

ztrace (Ztrace): the ztrace object to modify name (str): the new name color (tuple): the new color log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

enumerateSections(show_progress: bool = True, message: str = 'Loading series data...', series_states=None, breakable=True)[source]#

Allow iteration through the sections.

Proper use in a for loop: for snum, section in series.enumerateSections():

Params:

show_progress (bool): True if progress should be displayed message (str): the message to display by the progress bar series_states (dict): section number : SectionStates object (use with GUI for undo/redo) breakable (bool): True if sereis state is breakable

Returns:

(SeriesIterator): an iterable object for for loops

getAlignments() → list[source]#

Return a list of alignment names.

getAttr(name: str, attr_name: str, ztrace=False)[source]#

Get the attributes for an object in the series.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object attr_name (str): the name of the attribute to get

Returns:

the request attribute

getDefaultPaletteTraces() → list[source]#

Return the default palette trace list.

Returns:

(list): the list of the default palette traces

getDict() → dict[source]#

Convert series object into a dictionary.

Returns:

(dict): all of the compiled section data

getEmptyDict() → dict[source]#

Get an empty dictionary for a series object.

Returns:

(dict): the empty series dictionary

getFullHistory() → LogSet[source]#

Get all the logs for the series.

Returns:

(LogSet): the object containing the full history

getOption(option_name: str, get_default=False)[source]#

Get an option from the series (or computer)

Params:

option_name (str): the name of the option get_default (bool): True if only default should be returned

getRecentSegGroup() → str[source]#

Return the most recent segmentation group name.

Returns:

(str): the name of the most recent segmentation group

getZValues()[source]#

Return the z-coorindate for each section.

(Mainly for 3D use.)

Returns:

(dict): section number : z-value

getwdir() → str[source]#

Get the working directory of the series.

Returns:

(str): the directory containing the series

hideAllTraces(hidden=True, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Hide all traces in the entire series.

Params:

hidden (bool): True if traces are to be hidden series_states (dict): optional dict for GUI undo states log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

hideObjects(obj_names: list, hide=True, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Hide all traces of a set of objects throughout the series.

Params:

obj_names (list): the names of objects to hide hide (bool): True if object should be hidden series_states (dict): optional dict for GUI undo states log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

importBC(other, sections: list = None, log_event=True)[source]#

Import the brightness/contrast settings from another series.

Params:

other (Series): the other series to import from sections (list): the section numbers to import b/c for (default: all) log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

importFlags(other, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Import flags from another series.

Params:

other (Series): the series to import from series_states (SeriesStates): the series undo states from the GUI log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

importPalettes(other, log_event=True)[source]#

Import the palettes from another series.

Params:

other (Series): the series to import from log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

importTraces(other, srange: tuple = None, regex_filters: list = [], threshold: float = 0.95, flag_conflicts: bool = True, check_history: bool = True, favored: str = '', series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Import all the traces from another series.

Params:

other (Series): the series to import from srange (tuple): the range of sections to include in import (exclusive) regex_filters (list): the filters for the objects to import threshold (float): the overlap threshold remove_old_overlaps (bool): True if old traces overlapping new traces should be removed flag_conflicts (bool): True if conflicts should be flagged check_history (bool): True if history should be checked favored (str): the series that is favored in the case of a conflict (“self”, “other”, or “”) series_states (dict): optional dict of undo states for GUI log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

importTransforms(other, alignments: list, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Import transforms from another series.

Params:

other (series): the series to import transforms from alignments (list): the names of alignments to import log_event (bool): True if the event should be logged series_states (dict): optiona dict of undo states for GUI

importZtraces(other, regex_filters: list = [], series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Import all the ztraces from another series.

Params:

other (Series): the series to import from regex_filters (list): the filters for the objects to import series_states (SeriesStates): the series undo states from the GUI log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

insertSection(index: int, src: str, mag: float, thickness: float, log_event=True)[source]#

Create a new section.

Params:

index (int): the index of the new section src (str): the path to the image for the new section mag (float): the mag of the new section thickness (float): the thickness of the new section log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

isWelcomeSeries() → bool[source]#

Return True if self is the welcome series.

Returns:

(bool): True if this series is the wolcome series

loadSection(section_num: int) → Section[source]#

Load a section object.

Params:

section_num (int): the section number

Returns:

(Section): the section

modifyAlignments(alignment_dict: dict, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Modify the alignments (input from dialog).

Not suggested for use outside of GUI.

Params:

alignment_dict (dict): returned from the alignment dialog series_states (dict): optional dict of undo states for GUI log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

move(new_jser_fp: str, section: Section = None, b_section: Section = None)[source]#

Move/rename the series to its jser filepath.

Params:

new_jser_fp (str): the new location for the series section (Section): the section file being used (in GUI) b_section (Section): the secondary section file being used (in GUI)

new(series_name: str, mag: float, thickness: float)[source]#

Create a new blank series.

Params:

image_locations (list): the paths for each image series_name (str): user-entered series name mag (float): the microns per pixel for the series thickness (float): the section thickness

Returns:

(Series): the newly created series object

openJser()[source]#

Process the file containing all section and series information.

Params:

fp (str): the filepath to the jser

Returns:

(Series): the series object created from the jser

qsettings_defaults = {'3D_smoothing': 'humphrey', 'auto_merge': False, 'backup_dir': '', 'display_closest': True, 'fill_opacity': 0.2, 'find_zoom': 95.0, 'flag_color': [255, 0, 0], 'flag_columns': {'Color': True, 'Flag': True, 'Last Comment': True, 'Resolved': False, 'Section': True}, 'flag_name': '', 'flag_size': 14, 'grid': [1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1], 'knife_del_threshold': 1.0, 'left_handed': False, 'object_columns': {'Alignment': False, 'Comment': True, 'Count': False, 'Curate': False, 'Flat area': False, 'Groups': True, 'Last user': True, 'Range': True, 'Trace tags': False, 'Volume': False}, 'pointer': ['lasso', 'exc'], 'sampling_frame_grid': True, 'show_flags': 'unresolved', 'show_ztraces': True, 'trace_columns': {'Area': True, 'Index': False, 'Length': True, 'Radius': True, 'Tags': True}, 'user': 'michael'}#
removeAllTraceTags(obj_names: list, series_states=None, log_event=True)[source]#

Remove all tags from all traces on a set of objects.

Params:

obj_names (list): a list of object names series_states (dict): optional dict for GUI undo states log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

removeObjAttrs(name: str)[source]#

Delete all attrs associated with an object name.

(Automatically called when object is deleted.)

Params:

name (str): the name of the object

rename(new_name: str)[source]#

Rename the series.

Params:

new_name (str): the new name for the series

renameObjAttrs(old_name, new_name)[source]#

Change the attibutes for an object that was renamed.

(Automatically called when object is renamed.)

Params:

old_name (str): the original name of the object new_name (str): the new name for the object

reorderSections(d: dict = None, log_event=True)[source]#

Reorder the sections.

Params:

d (dict): old_snum : new_snum for every section log_event (bool): True if event should be logged

save()[source]#

Save file into json.

saveJser(save_fp: str = None, close: bool = False)[source]#

Save the jser file.

Params:

save_fp (str): the optional override filepath to save the jser file close (bool): True if sereis should be closed after saving

setAttr(name: str, attr_name: str, value, ztrace=False)[source]#

Set the attributes for an object in the series.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object attr_name (str): the name of the attribute to set value: the value to set for the attributes

setCuration(names: list, cr_status: str, assign_to: str = '')[source]#

Set the curation status for a set of objects.

Params:

names (list): the object names to mark as curated cr_status(str): the curation state to set asign_to (str): the user to assign to if Needs Curation

setOption(option_name: str, value)[source]#

Set an option

Params:

options_name (str): the name of the option value: the value to set the option as

updateCurationFromHistory()[source]#

Update curation status of all objects from the history.

updateJSON()[source]#

Add missing attributes to the series JSON.

(Updates the dictionary in place)

Params:

series_data (dict): the JSON data to update

property user#
class SeriesIterator(series: Series, show_progress: bool, message: str, series_states, breakable=True)[source]#

Bases: object

getDateTime()[source]#

modules.datatypes.series_data module#

class ObjectData[source]#

Bases: object

addTrace(trace: Trace, section: Section, series)[source]#

Add a trace to the object data.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to add section (Section): the section containing the trace series (Series): the series containing the trace

clearSection(snum: int)[source]#

Clear the traces on a specific section.

Params:

snum (int): the section number to clear

isEmpty() → bool[source]#

Return True of object data is empty.

class SeriesData(series)[source]#

Bases: object

addTrace(trace: Trace, section: Section)[source]#

Add trace data to the existing object.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to add section (Section): the section containing the trace

Returns:

(bool): True if a new object was just created

clearSection(snum: int)[source]#

Clear the object data for a speicified section.

Params:

snum (int): the section number

getCount(obj_name: str) → int[source]#

Get the number of traces associated with the object.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to retrieve data for

getEnd(obj_name: str) → int[source]#

Get the last section of the object.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to retrieve data for

getFlagCount() → int[source]#

Get the number of flags in the series.

getFlatArea(obj_name: str) → float[source]#

Get the flat area of the object.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to retrieve data for

getStart(obj_name: str) → int[source]#

Get the first section of the object.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to retrieve data for

getTags(obj_name: str) → set[source]#

Get the tags associated with an object.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to retrieve data for

getTraceData(name: str, snum: int) → list[source]#

Get the list of trace data objects.

Params:

name (str): the name of the object/trace snum (int): the section number

Returns:

(list): the list of TraceData objects

getVolume(obj_name: str) → float[source]#

Get the volume of the object.

Params:

obj_name (str): the name of the object to retrieve data for

getZtraceDist(ztrace_name: str) → float[source]#

Get the distance of a ztrace.

Params:

ztrace_name (str): the name of the ztrace to retrieve data for

getZtraceEnd(ztrace_name: str) → int[source]#

Get the last section of a ztrace.

Params:

ztrace_name (str): the name of the ztrace to retrieve data for

getZtraceStart(ztrace_name: str) → int[source]#

Get the first section of a ztrace.

Params:

ztrace_name (str): the name of the ztrace to retrieve data for

refresh()[source]#

Completely refresh the series data.

updateSection(section: Section, update_traces=False, all_traces=True, log_events=True)[source]#

Update the existing section data.

Params:

section (Section): the section with data to update update_traces (bool): True if all traces should also be updated all_traces (bool): True if all traces on the section should be updated IF NO TRACES HAVE BEEN MARKED AS MODIFIED log_events (bool): True if events (creating and deleting objects) should be logged

class TraceData(trace: Trace, tform: Transform)[source]#

Bases: object

getArea()[source]#
getLength()[source]#
getRadius()[source]#
getTags()[source]#

modules.datatypes.trace module#

class Trace(name: str, color: tuple, closed=True)[source]#

Bases: object

add(point: tuple)[source]#

Add a point to the trace.

Params:

point (tuple): a coordinate pair

addTag(tag: str)[source]#

Set the tag for the trace:

Params:

tag (str): the tag for the trace

centerAtOrigin()[source]#

Centers the trace at the origin (ignores transformations).

copy()[source]#

Create a copy of the trace object.

Returns:

(Trace): a copy of the object

fromList(name: str = None)[source]#

Create a trace object from a dictionary.

Params:

list (dict): the list trace data name (str): the name of the trace

Returns:

(Trace) a Trace object constructed from the dictionary data

fromXMLObj(xml_image_tform: Transform = None)[source]#

Create a trace from an xml contour object.

Params:

xml_trace (XMLContour): the xml contour object xml_image_tform (XMLTransform): the xml image transform object

Returns:

(Trace) the trace object

getBounds(tform: Transform = None) → tuple[source]#

Get the most extreme coordinates for the trace.

Params:

tform (Transform): optional parameter to find extremeties of transformed trace

Returns:

(float) min x value (float) min y value (float) max x value (float) max y value

getCentroid(tform: Transform = None) → tuple[source]#

Get the centroid of the trace.

Params:

tform (Transform)

getList(include_name=True) → dict[source]#

Return the trace data as a dictionary.

Params:

include_name (bool): True if name should be included in dict

Returns:

(list) list containing the trace data

getMidpoint(tform: Transform = None) → tuple[source]#

Get the midpoint of the trace (avg of extremes).

Params:

tform (Transform): transform to apply to calculation

Returns:

(tuple) x, y of midpoint

getOverlapRatio(other)[source]#

Get the amount of intersection between two traces.

Params:

other (Trace): the trace to compare against

getRadius(tform: Transform = None) → float[source]#

Get the distance from the centroid of the trace to its farthest point.

Params:

tform (Transform): the transform to apply to the points

Returns:

(float): the radius of the trace

getStretched(w: float, h: float)[source]#

Get the trace stretched to a specific w and h.

Params:

w (float): the width of the resulting trace h (float): the height of the resulting trace

Returns:

(Trace): the resulting trace

getXMLObj(xml_image_tform: Transform = None, legacy_format: bool = False)[source]#

Returns the trace data as an XML object. Params:

xml_image_tform (XMLTransform): the xml image transform object

Returns:

(XMLContour) the trace as an xml contour object or (Str)

isSameTrace(other) → bool[source]#

Check if traces have the same name, color, and points.

Params:

other (Trace): the trace to compare

Returns:

(bool) whether or not the traces are the same

magScale(prev_mag: float, new_mag: float)[source]#

Scale the trace to magnification changes.

Params:

prev_mag (float): the previous magnification new_mag (float): the new magnification

mergeTags(other)[source]#

Merge the tags of two traces.

Params:

other (Trace): the trace to merge tags with

overlaps(other, threshold=0.99)[source]#

Check if trace points overlap.

Params:

other (Trace): the trace to compare threshold (float): the threshold overlap ratio to define overlapping (exclusive)

Returns:

(bool): whether or not trace traces overlap

reshape(new_points: float, tform: Transform = None)[source]#

Resize a trace beased on its radius

Params:

new_points (float): the new points for the trace

resize(new_radius: float)[source]#

Resize a trace beased on its radius

Params:

new_radius (float): the new radius for the trace

setHidden(hidden=True)[source]#

Set whether the trace is hidden.

Params:

hidden (bool): whether the trace is hidden

convertMode(arg)[source]#

Translate between Reconstruct and PyReconstruct fill modes.

getLegacyRadius(trace: Trace)[source]#

Get the legacy radius for a palette trace.

modules.datatypes.transform module#

class Transform(tform_list: list)[source]#

Bases: object

copy()[source]#

Returns a copy of the transform.

property det#
equals(other)[source]#

Compare two transforms

Params:

other (Transform): the other transform

estimateTform(pts2)[source]#

Estimate the transform that converts pts1 to pts2.

Params:

pts1 (list): the list of original points pts2 (list): the list of points to transform into

fromQTransform()[source]#

Get a Transform object from a QTransform object.

getLinear()[source]#
getList() → list[source]#

Get the tform list numbers.

Returns:

(list): the six-number transform

getQTransform() → QTransform[source]#

Get the transform as a QTransform object.

Returns:

(QTransform): the QTransform object

imageTransform()[source]#

Get the transform object as it should apply to images.

Returns:

(Transform): the image-style transform

inverted()[source]#

Return the inverted transform.

Returns:

(Transform): the inverted transform

magScale(prev_mag: float, new_mag: float)[source]#

Scale the transform to magnification changes.

Params:

prev_mag (float): the previous magnification new_mag (float): the new magnification

map(*args, inverted=False)[source]#

Apply the transform to a single point or a list of points.

Params:

(tuple): an x, y coordinate pair to transform OR (list): a list of points to transform

Returns:

(tuple) OR (list): the transformed point or points

modules.datatypes.ztrace module#

class Ztrace(name: str, color: tuple, points: list = [])[source]#

Bases: object

copy()[source]#

Return a copy of the ztrace object.

dictFromXMLObj()[source]#

Create a trace from an xml contour object.

Params:

xml_trace (XMLContour): the xml contour object xml_image_tform (XMLTransform): the xml image transform object

Returns:

(Trace) the trace object

fromDict(d)[source]#

Create the object from a dictionary.

Params:

d (dict): the dictionary representation of the object

getDict() → dict[source]#

Get a dictionary representation of the object.

Returns:

(dict): the dictionary representation of the object

getDistance(series)[source]#

Get the distance of the z-trace.

Params:

series (Series): the series containing the ztrace

Returns:

(float): the distance of the ztrace

getEnd()[source]#

Get the last section of the ztrace.

getSectionData(series, section)[source]#

Get all the ztrace points on a section.

Params:

series (Series): the series object section (Section): the main section object

Returns:

(list): list of points (list): list of lines between points

getStart()[source]#

Get the first section of the ztrace.

getXMLObj(series)[source]#

Convert the ztrace into an XML object.

Params:

series (Series): the series containing the ztrace

Returns:

(XMLZContour): the XML zcontour object

overlaps(other)[source]#

Check if the ztraces have the same set of points.

smooth(series, smooth=10)[source]#

Smooth z-trace (based on legacy Reconstruct algorithm).

Params:

series (Series): the series object (contains transform data) smooth (int): the smoothing factor
